title: Let's Explore the City! Game Zone
source: Generated from approved unit runtime and linked master dictionary

games.title: Meaning Match
games.description: Match each unit word to its approved child-friendly meaning.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
rounds.explanation: library: A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: A busy place where people buy and sell food and other things.
rounds.explanation: market: A busy place where people buy and sell food and other things.

rounds.prompt: Which word means: Words that tell you which way to go.
rounds.explanation: directions: Words that tell you which way to go.

games.title: Spelling Builder
games.description: Choose letter tiles to build important unit words.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A train that runs in tunnels below the city streets.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A flying machine that can go straight up and hover in one place.

rounds.prompt: Build the word that matches the clue.
rounds.clue: A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.

games.title: Sentence Puzzle
games.description: Put unit words into the correct sentence order.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: I go to the library to borrow books about the sea.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: We took the underground to the museum.

rounds.prompt: Build the complete model sentence.
rounds.answer: Mum buys fruit and fish at the market.

games.title: Language Choice
games.description: Choose the form that makes the unit language accurate.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence uses the present simple correctly?
choices: The ferry take people across the river.
The ferry takes people across the river.
The ferry taking people across the river.
The ferry taked people across the river.
rounds.answer: The ferry takes people across the river.
rounds.explanation: One ferry is an it, so the verb needs the -s ending: takes. The other three leave out the -s, use an -ing form, or invent a past form.

rounds.prompt: Which sentence talks about the future?
choices: Tomorrow we will ride the ferry.
Yesterday we went to the aquarium.
We ate fruit at the market.
Last week we saw the sharks.
rounds.answer: Tomorrow we will ride the ferry.
rounds.explanation: Tomorrow and will both point forward. The other three sentences use the past verbs went, ate and saw.

rounds.prompt: Your friend says, “I like the library.” Which reply agrees politely?
rounds.explanation: “I do too!” agrees. “I don't.” disagrees, “I no like it.” is not correct English, and “Why do you?” asks a question instead of giving an opinion.

games.title: Reading Detective
games.description: Use unit evidence to solve each reading clue.

rounds.prompt: What does the poem tell you to look around at when you stand at the zebra crossing?
choices: They took the bus into the city.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: City places and city faces.

rounds.prompt: The schedule calls one animal clever and another one hungry. Which animal is which?
choices: She smelled fresh bread and sweet mangoes, and people were buying fish, fruit and bright cloth.
The octopus is clever and the shark is hungry.
rounds.answer: The octopus is clever and the shark is hungry.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: The octopus is clever and the shark is hungry.

rounds.prompt: Who did the children meet near the shopping center, and what was his problem?
choices: She smelled fresh bread and sweet mangoes, and people were buying fish, fruit and bright cloth.
An old man who was lost and needed to find the hospital, because his daughter was there with a new baby.
Because the words are shouted, and shouting shows there is only a short, safe moment left to cross.
rounds.answer: An old man who was lost and needed to find the hospital, because his daughter was there with a new baby.
rounds.explanation: Review the unit text evidence: An old man who was lost and needed to find the hospital, because his daughter was there with a new baby.

games.title: Speaking Quest
games.description: Listen, record, review and submit useful unit language.

rounds.prompt: Speaking 1 — Talk About City Places
Speaking Game — Mystery Animal
Speaking 5 — Aquarium Expert

games.title: Word Order Race
games.description: Arrange unit language in a clear, accurate order.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: We take the bus to school every morning.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: A kind woman gave us directions to the market.

rounds.prompt: Put this unit sentence in order.
rounds.answer: Penguins waddle from side to side when they walk.

games.title: Definition Dash
games.description: Choose the approved meaning before moving to the next clue.

rounds.prompt: What does 'traffic' mean in this unit?
choices: All the cars, buses and lorries moving on the roads.
A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
A large building with many different shops inside it.
rounds.answer: All the cars, buses and lorries moving on the roads.
rounds.explanation: traffic: All the cars, buses and lorries moving on the roads.

rounds.prompt: What does 'octopus' mean in this unit?
choices: A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
A large building with many different shops inside it.
A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.
rounds.answer: A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.
rounds.explanation: octopus: A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.

rounds.prompt: What does 'shopping center' mean in this unit?
choices: A train that runs in tunnels below the city streets.
A large building with many different shops inside it.
A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
rounds.answer: A large building with many different shops inside it.
rounds.explanation: shopping center: A large building with many different shops inside it.

games.title: Word Type Power
games.description: Identify how each vocabulary word works in English.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'map'?
rounds.explanation: map is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'turtle'?
rounds.explanation: turtle is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

rounds.prompt: What type of word is 'Ferris wheel'?
rounds.explanation: Ferris wheel is used as a noun in this dictionary sense.

games.title: Memory Pairs
games.description: Reveal tiles and connect each word with its meaning.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 1.
pairs: A train that runs in tunnels below the city streets.
A boat that carries people and cars across water.
A busy place where people buy and sell food and other things.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 2.
pairs: A flying machine that can go straight up and hover in one place.
A drawing that shows you where places are.
Words that tell you which way to go.

rounds.prompt: Match vocabulary set 3.
pairs: A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.
A slow sea animal with a hard shell on its back.
A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.

games.title: Question Quest
games.description: Read each unit question carefully and choose the best answer.

rounds.prompt: We took the ____ across the river to the other side.
rounds.explanation: A ferry is the boat that carries people across water. A market and a library are buildings, and a shark is a sea animal.

rounds.prompt: Which spelling is correct?
rounds.explanation: Ferris wheel has a double r, a single s, and wheel is spelled with wh and a double e.

rounds.prompt: How many arms does an octopus have?
rounds.explanation: Reading 2 and the library book in the story both say that an octopus has eight arms.

games.title: Unit Mission
games.description: Finish with a mixed challenge across the whole unit.

rounds.prompt: What does the word library mean?
choices: A boat that carries people and cars across water.
A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
All the cars, buses and lorries moving on the roads.
A sea animal with eight long arms and no bones at all.
rounds.answer: A building where you can read books and borrow them to take home.
rounds.explanation: A library is full of books to read and to borrow. The other three options describe a ferry, traffic and an octopus.

rounds.prompt: Which word means a large building with many different shops inside it?
rounds.explanation: A shopping center holds many shops under one roof. A penguin is a bird, a helicopter flies, and directions are words that tell you the way.

rounds.prompt: Which of these travels below the city streets?
rounds.explanation: The underground runs through tunnels below the streets. The ferry travels on water, the Ferris wheel turns in a park, and the helicopter flies above the city.
